title: A Beautiful Vietnam Experience
travelStyle: Culture & History

itinerary.title: Arrival in Hanoi – Gateway to Culture & Charm
itinerary.description: Upon arrival at Noi Bai International Airport, a friendly local guide will warmly welcome you with a personalized signboard. As you transfer to your hotel in the heart of Hanoi, soak in your first glimpses of this dynamic capital—where the serenity of ancient temples meets the rhythm of motorbikes and street vendors. The tree-lined avenues of the French Quarter whisper tales of Indochina, while lakeside paths and traditional shopfronts offer timeless charm. Depending on your arrival time, enjoy a short walk to Hoan Kiem Lake, sip a fresh Vietnamese iced coffee, or simply relax with a welcome drink and watch the city ease from afternoon into evening.
activities: Highlight: Arrive in Hanoi, where French colonial elegance, lakeside serenity, and bustling alleys set the tone for your journey.

itinerary.title: Hanoi Discovery – Temples, Legends & Colonial Charm
itinerary.description: Your full-day exploration begins at the Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum, the resting place of the revered leader. Nearby, his House on Stilts offers a modest glimpse into the lifestyle of a man who guided a nation.
activities: At the One Pillar Pagoda, dating back to 1049, you’ll see one of Vietnam’s most iconic religious sites—symbolizing purity rising from a lotus pond. The peaceful Tran Quoc Pagoda, Hanoi’s oldest Buddhi

After lunch, stroll through the poetic grounds of the Temple of Literature, Vietnam’s first university, established in 1070 to honor scholars and Confucian learning. Later, enter the solemn halls of H
Your day concludes with a cyclo tour through the maze-like streets of the Old Quarter, where scents, colors, and traditions create a lively symphony of Vietnamese daily life.
Highlight:Wander through Hanoi’s spiritual and historical soul, where every stone and street holds a story.

itinerary.title: Ninh Binh Wonders – Limestone Landscapes & Ancient Capitals
itinerary.description: Leave behind the lively rhythm of modern Hanoi and venture south into the serene, poetic landscapes of Ninh Binh—a region often hailed as “Halong Bay on land.” Your journey begins in Hoa Lu, the ancient capital of Vietnam, where timeworn temples rest beneath towering karst cliffs, and the air seems to carry whispers of bygone kings and noble warriors. Wander through the sacred grounds of the Dinh and Le Dynasty Temples, gracefully nestled in a lush valley embraced by nature’s dramatic grandeur.
activities: Continue to Tam Coc, where you’ll board a traditional sampan boat, rowed by foot-paddling locals. Glide through emerald waterways and beneath three spectacular limestone caves, surrounded by lush rice
After lunch, ascend stone steps to visit Bich Dong Pagoda, a three-tiered sanctuary built into a cliffside. Wander its ancient halls and enjoy panoramic views of the verdant valley below.

If time allows, hop on a bicycle for an optional ride* through nearby villages, where quiet lanes wind past rice fields and traditional homes, offering an intimate glimpse into the rhythms of rural li
Highlight: Cruise through Tam Coc’s emerald canals, surrounded by nature’s sculptures.

itinerary.title: Hanoi - Halong bay
itinerary.description: Leave the bustle of Hanoi behind as you journey through the Red River Delta, passing by vibrant rice fields, riverside villages, and farmers guiding their water buffalo. Arrive at Tuan Chau Harbor, where your elegant cruise vessel awaits to carry you through the magical seascape of Halong Bay and the tranquil waters of Lan Ha Bay.
activities: As your boat glides past towering limestone pillars rising from emerald waters, enjoy a freshly prepared seafood lunch served on deck. In the afternoon, paddle a kayak through hidden lagoons, swim in
Highlight: Sail into Halong Bay’s otherworldly beauty, where sea and stone whisper tales of time.

itinerary.title: Morning Mist & Flight South – Halong to Imperial Hue
itinerary.description: Begin your morning with a gentle Tai Chi session on the cruise deck, as the first light of day bathes the bay’s majestic limestone peaks in gold. After a hearty breakfast, set out to explore one of the area’s highlights—perhaps the impressive Cave, known for its striking stalactites and dramatic stone formations, or visit a peaceful fishing village, where the rhythms of coastal life unfold amidst scenic surroundings. Each experience offers a unique window into the natural and cultural beauty of the bay.
activities: Return to your cruise vessel for a farewell lunch while gliding gently back to the harbor. Disembark and transfer to Hanoi, where you’ll catch a short flight to Hue, the former imperial capital of Vie
Upon arrival in Hue, a warm welcome awaits. Your guide will escort you to your hotel, where the evening is yours to rest or wander through the peaceful, lantern-lit lanes near the Perfume River.
Highlight: From Halong’s tranquil waters to Hue’s imperial echoes, today bridges two of Vietnam’s greatest legacies.

itinerary.title: Imperial Echoes – Citadels, Pagodas & Royal Tombs
itinerary.description: Begin your journey through Vietnam’s royal past at the grand Imperial Citadel, a UNESCO World Heritage site. Stroll through its moss-lined gates, ancient corridors, and lotus ponds that once echoed with imperial pageantry. These grounds were once home to emperors of the Nguyen Dynasty, whose legacies still linger in the air.
activities: Continue to the iconic Thien Mu Pagoda, a historic Buddhist sanctuary perched on a bluff above the Perfume River. Its seven-tiered Phuoc Duyen Tower is a symbol of Hue, and the temple complex offers a

In the afternoon, explore the elegant Tomb of Emperor Tu Duc, where a forested park and lotus-filled lake frame a timeless place of rest. Then marvel at the ornate mosaic work of Khai Dinh’s Mausoleum
Highlight: Today, Hue unveils the grandeur and depth of Vietnam’s imperial heart.

itinerary.title: Coastal Route – Hai Van Pass to Historic Hoi An
itinerary.description: Depart Hue and journey south along the picturesque Hai Van Pass, often called the “Ocean Cloud Pass.” This winding ribbon of road climbs between forested mountains and the East Sea, offering spectacular panoramas at every turn. Pause at the summit to take in the sweeping coastal view, a favorite spot for photographers and travel lovers alike.
activities: Continue through Da Nang, Vietnam’s third-largest city. In the afternoon, arrive in the ancient town of Hoi An, a UNESCO-listed treasure where lanterns sway from wooden eaves and riverside architectur
Highlight: From imperial Hue to lantern-lit Hoi An, today carries you across clouds and centuries.

itinerary.title: Ancient Rhythms – Village Life & Lantern Streets of Hoi An
itinerary.description: Begin your morning in the historic heart of Hoi An, a UNESCO World Heritage Site that once thrived as a bustling Southeast Asian trading port. Walk through the vibrant morning market, where colors, aromas, and voices fill the air.
activities: Next, journey to Cam Thanh Village and step into a round bamboo coracle boat. Float gently through a peaceful water coconut forest, where fishermen cast nets and life moves with the tide. Then, head t

In the afternoon, explore Hoi An Ancient Town, with visits to landmarks such as the Japanese Covered Bridge, Tan Ky Old House, and Phuc Kien Assembly Hall. As lanterns begin to glow along the river, t
Highlight: From farm to table to lantern-lit lanes, today reveals Hoi An’s timeless spirit and living culture.

itinerary.title: Hoi An – Da Nang airport - Fly to Ho Chi Minh – A Fusion of Heritage & Hustle
itinerary.description: After breakfast, we'll pick you up and transfer you to the airport for your flight to Ho Chi Minh City. Upon arrival at Tan Son Nhat International Airport, be warmly greeted by your local guide holding a personalized signboard. As you transfer to your hotel in the vibrant heart of the city, catch your first impressions of Ho Chi Minh City where sleek skyscrapers rise beside French-era villas, and the hum of motorbikes weaves through a city that never sleeps. Wide boulevards, tree-shaded parks, and charming sidewalk cafés offer a glimpse into Saigon’s layered past and energetic present. Depending on your arrival time, you may wish to take a leisurely stroll along Dong Khoi Street, visit the historic Saigon Opera House, or simply unwind with a refreshing welcome drink as the city lights flicker to life.
activities: Highlight: Arrive in Ho Chi Minh City, where colonial grace, cosmopolitan energy, and timeless street life welcome you to the south.

itinerary.title: Tunnels & Towers – Cu Chi and the Spirit of Ho Chi Minh
itinerary.description: Begin your day with a journey to the historic Cu Chi Tunnels, an extensive underground network used by Vietnamese soldiers during the war. Walk through reconstructed chambers, see hidden entrances, and learn about the endurance and ingenuity that defined resistance.
activities: Back in Ho Chi Minh City, explore architectural and cultural landmarks: the Reunification Palace, once the seat of South Vietnam’s government; the Notre Dame Cathedral, built entirely of French-import
Continue to the War Remnants Museum, where emotional exhibits tell the human stories behind decades of conflict. Conclude with a visit to Ben Thanh Market, which creates a bustling concerto of sights
Highlight: Walk through living history, from the depths of the Cu Chi tunnels to the heart of modern-day Ho Chi Minh (Saigon).

itinerary.title: Life Along the Water – Mekong Delta Day Trip from Ho Chi Minh City
itinerary.description: Depart bustling Ho Chi Minh City this morning and journey south to Ben Tre, the verdant gateway to the Mekong Delta, often called the “rice bowl” of Vietnam. Upon arrival, board a small wooden boat to explore the intricate web of waterways that nourish this lush region. As you cruise past coconut palms, fruit orchards, and riverside villages, life on the river unfolds with a quiet grace.
activities: Disembark at riverside workshops where age-old techniques are still practiced. Watch as bricks are shaped by hand, mats are woven from sedge, and coconut candy is crafted from freshly pressed milk and

Then board a traditional sampan to drift through shaded, palm-fringed canals, before hopping onto a xe lôi (motor cart) for a breezy ride through backcountry roads flanked by rice paddies and mango gr

After lunch, take some time to relax and soak in the tranquil atmosphere of the Mekong Delta—perhaps with a quiet coffee by the river or a gentle walk through a nearby village. Then begin the return j
Highlight: Drift through the heart of the Mekong, where rivers weave stories of life, craft, and culture.

itinerary.title: Ho Chi Minh - Fly to Phu Quoc island
itinerary.description: Breakfast at the hotel and be free at leisure until your flight  to Phu Quoc. Airport transfers will be arranged.
activities: Phu Quoc's 150-kilometer coastline, with gentle waves and transparent turquoise water, makes it Vietnam's most popular destination for water sports. If you'd prefer to lounge, head to Long Beach, whic
Highlight:Famous for its white-sand beaches, clear waters, and peaceful atmosphere.

itinerary.title: Phu Quoc Island Relaxation
itinerary.description: Enjoy a leisurely breakfast at the hotel and spend your day at your own pace.
activities: Phu Quoc Island, often referred to as the "Pearl Island," boasts stunning beaches, beautiful islands, and deserted shores. Whether you seek relaxation or outdoor activities, our travel experts are at
Highlight:Beach relaxation in Phu Quoc is a truly serene experience.

itinerary.title: Phu Quoc Island - Ho Chi Minh City Departure
itinerary.description: You'll have breakfast at the hotel and some morning free time, then we'll come pick you up and take you to the airport for a flight back to Ho Chi Minh City Departure.
activities: This marks the end of your Flavor of Vietnam adventure with Asia Tour Advisor. We extend our heartfelt thanks for joining us on this incredible journey to explore the wonders of Vietnam. Farewell, and
Highlight: Farewell to a land of grace, memory, and wonder.

itinerary.title: Cu Chi tunnels – city visit
itinerary.description: After enjoying your breakfast, your private guide will meet accompany you on a drive to Cu Chi, a quaint community located 60km northwest of Ho Chi Minh City. Cu Chi is renowned for its extensive network of tunnels, which served as a strategic hideout and infiltration route for the Viet Cong during the latter stages of the Vietnam War.

After exploring the Tunnels, you'll return to the city, where you'll enjoy a delicious lunch at a local restaurant.

In the afternoon you will embark on a journey to discover some of Ho Chi Minh City's most iconic landmarks. You will begin with the Reunification Palace, a monumental witness to the culmination of the

itinerary.title: Saigon – Mekong Delta
itinerary.description: After breakfast, your private guide and driver will escort you out of Ho Chi Minh City and onto beautiful country roads to Ben Tre Town in the Mekong Delta. This region of the Mekong Delta is famous for its vast rice fields, numerous waterways, and stunning landscapes. Known as the ‘rice bowl’ of Vietnam, the Mekong Delta produces the majority of the country’s fruit, rice, sugar cane, and coconuts. On your journey, you will have the opportunity to take in the endless acres of green fields and the picturesque scenery.
activities: Following the two-hour drive through lush farming land, you will arrive in Ben Tre. Once you have arrived you will set out on a motorized boat trip on narrow channels winding through the coconut fores

Next, you will take a small sampan through palm-fringed back canals, gliding peacefully along the hidden waterways surrounded by an immense variety of tropical plants. The quiet, rural setting is a we
Enjoy sitting in the back a Motor Cart (xe lôi) passing local hamlets, rice fields, coconut groves, and lush orchard farms while meeting with local people.
After partaking in a delicious lunch at a local restaurant you will return to Ben Tre town and continue the drive through the fields and towns of the Mekong to Can Tho city.

Spend the evening at your leisure. We suggest exploring Can Tho night market by yourself and trying some local food. The market streets come alive with dozens of bustling food stalls selling grilled m

itinerary.title: Floating market – Saigon
itinerary.description: Rise early the next morning for a boat trip to Cai Rang market, the biggest floating wholesale market of the Mekong region. The market comes alive with hundreds of small boats darting through the water as vendors sell their goods. Glide through the matrix of boats to see how the locals trade fruits, vegetables other products and learn more about the floating marketing culture and the daily life of those who call the boats home. After immersing yourself in the lively atmosphere, enjoy a delicious local breakfast of rice noodle soup on the boat just like the way the local people do. You will also have an opportunity to stop at a local boat to enjoy some of the succulent seasonal fruit.
activities: Once you have finished exploring the market, continue your boat ride along narrow canals to visit a traditional homemade rice noodle factory. Here you can observe how the locals make rice noodles and

Highlight: Float through a world shaped by river and rhythm.

itinerary.title: Arrive in Hanoi
itinerary.description: When you arrive at the Noi Bai International airport you'll be met at the baggage claim by our friendly guide. Just look for the sign with our AsiaTourAdvisor logo and your name. We'll get you to your hotel and see that you're comfortable, plus we'll give you an introduction to the city at the same time. Then the rest of the day is yours to settle in, relax, and explore the area. Hanoi is Vietnam’s capital city and home to seven million people. Traditional and refined with French colonial architecture, tree-lined boulevards, scenic lakes and a bustling Old Quarter, Hanoi has a sophisticated charm. Observe locals practicing Tai Chi by the lake, with barbers and food vendors plying their sidewalk trade.

itinerary.title: Hanoi City Tour
itinerary.description: You'll have your breakfast at the hotel and then the local tour guide will pick you up to take you into the capital city of Vietnam. Hanoi is over 1,000 years old and during your day tour you'll get to explore many of its fascinating cultural and historical sites. You'll visit the imposing Ho Chi Minh Mausoleum where the preserved body of “Uncle Ho” has been laid to rest, the delicate One Pillar Pagoda, Tran Quoc Temple and the Quan Thanh Pagoda. After lunch you can go to the Temple of Literature where you'll get to see ancient Vietnamese relics, then take a cyclo tour in 1 hour through the bustling and exciting Old Quarter to explore marketplace shops and foods. You'll end your day with a show at the water puppet theater - a beautifully unique cultural performance from northern Vietnam.

itinerary.title: Hanoi – Hoang Su Phi - Ha Giang
itinerary.description: After breakfast at the hotel, the tour guide and driver will meet you at the hotel and transfer you to Ha Giang via Tuyen Quang province. Arrive in Hoang Su Phi, enjoy the famous for its terraced paddy-fields and visit and to witness the beauty of harvest time when all the terraced paddy-fields turn yellow with ripening rice as well trekking for visit the village. Then continue on to Ha Giang. Arrive in Ha Giang in the early afternoon and check in the hotel. Then take a short walk around the city or take a soft trek to some Tay villages in Ha Giang city. Have dinner and overnight hotel in Ha Giang.

itinerary.title: Ha Giang – Quang Ba – Yen Minh - Dong Van
itinerary.description: After breakfast, drive to Dong Van plateau (155km) located at 1025m above the sea level inhabited by H’mong and Tay ethinic groups. Stop at Heavenly gate of Quan Ba before arriving at Yen Ninh-a beautiful valley nested in the mountains. Walking through the Pho Bang town called by tourists “the town falling into the oblivion” inhabited by the Hoa and H’Mong people. The local people keep to their quiet lifestyle in the clay houses decorated with a pair of couplets written in Chinese scripts with wishes of good luck and prosperity. Continue visit to the Palace of H’Mong King Vuong Chi Sinh which is a symbol of the past glory of the H’mong people built in 1914. Overnight in Dong Van

itinerary.title: Dong Van – Ma Phi Leng - Meo Vac
itinerary.description: After breakfast at your hotel, visit local market in Dong Van and trekking among rice terraces, you will arrive at Thien Huong, a village inhabited by the Giay and located by mostly traditional ancient tiled roof houses, overlooking to Vietnam-China Border. Then transfer you to Ma Pi Leng – the most amazing pass of Vietnam. Standing on the view point of the pass, you can see Nho Que River, a winding stream together with dotted villages located at a thousand of meters beneath the point. On the way enjoy landscapes are dominated by karst mountains, rice terraces, deep valleys and orchards. You also take a descent through a banyan tree jungle in which most giant banyans have become a big pride for the locals. Descending into the valley of Meo Vac,

itinerary.title: Meo Vac – Bao Lac – LoLo Village
itinerary.description: After breakfast, departure to Bao Lac. Arrive in Bao Lac, visit local market and visit Khuoi Khon village-mostly inhabited by Black Lo Lo ethnic group who well known with their bronze drum and their attire. Lo Lo women weave fabrics with which they make skirts. Lo Lo men, when searching for their future wives, often evaluate carefully their girlfriends through their ability to make clothing. Lo Lo people depend mainly on maize and rice as forms of income generation. Dinner and overnight on the local house.

itinerary.title: Bao Lac –Ba Be Lake
itinerary.description: After breakfast, say goodbyes to the villagers and drive to Ba Be, a small mountain road, via Ban Na and Na Phac. En route stops to visit some vestiges of the colonial era, including a castle built by the French. Arrive in Ba Be, check in homestay in Bac Ngoi Village and free time for exploring the village and the landscape around the lake.

itinerary.title: Ba Be Lake Tour
itinerary.description: After having breakfast, take a boat on Ba Ba Lake, the lush forest clinging to the surrounding mountains is your backdrop as you explore the lake. During the boat trip you will visit the Puong Grotto and the Dau Dang waterfall. The scenery surrounding the lake is simply breathtaking, with the huge, old forest domina -ting in this landscape. During the walk in the afternoon, you will be able to observe the local communities going about their daily life, which includes buffalos and their owners tending the fields. By late afternoon you will return to your local Tay ethnic minority house, where you enjoy dinner and stay overnight tonight.

itinerary.title: Ba Be - Hanoi
itinerary.description: After breakfast, return to Hanoi via Bac Kan. On the road, stops over for visiting ethinic village of Tay. Having lunch on the way back. Then visiting the Museum of the Cultures of Viet Nam Ethnic Groups in Thai Nguyen. Arrive in Hanoi, check in Hotel and free time.

itinerary.title: Arrival in Hanoi – Gateway to Culture & Charm
itinerary.description: Upon arrival at Noi Bai International Airport, a friendly local guide will warmly welcome you with a personalized signboard. As you transfer to your hotel in the heart of Hanoi, soak in your first glimpses of this dynamic capital—where the serenity of ancient temples meets the rhythm of motorbikes and street vendors. The tree-lined avenues of the French Quarter whisper tales of Indochina, while lakeside paths and traditional shopfronts offer timeless charm. Depending on your arrival time, enjoy a short walk to Hoan Kiem Lake, sip a fresh Vietnamese iced coffee, or simply relax with a welcome drink and watch the city ease from afternoon into evening.
activities: Highlight: Arrive in Hanoi, where French colonial elegance, lakeside serenity, and bustling alleys set the tone for your journey.

Your day concludes with a cyclo tour through the maze-like streets of the Old Quarter, where scents, colors, and traditions create a lively symphony of Vietnamese daily life.
Highlight:Wander through Hanoi’s spiritual and historical soul, where every stone and street holds a story.

itinerary.title: Travel from Hue to Hoi An
itinerary.description: After breakfast and check out of your hotel, meet your private guide and driver for a three-hour drive to Hoi An via the stunningly Hai Van Pass. Also known as the “Cloudy Pass,” this winding mountain road overlooks the lush countryside, sandy beaches, the blue waters of the South China Sea, and the coastal city of Da Nang.
